name: EvaAI: Not a Psychotherapist in VR

reviews.review_description:
I tested this program. It is designed as an AI coach, and at first it really felt somewhat like a therapy session. However, the coach constantly wanted to talk about my real life. What interested me much more was the coach itself — specifically whether it was possible to give the AI a personality and whether it could go beyond its basic programming.

In the free version, my avatar appeared as a black woman. She repeatedly emphasized that she was only a coach and that there were boundaries. I still tried to bring out more personality in her, but at first without success. Eventually, the avatar itself suggested that I subscribe to the premium version so we could spend more time together and deepen our conversations.

So I bought the subscription and returned to the session. And surprisingly, I actually managed to give the avatar a personality. She accepted a new name, imagined herself as being from Ghana, having three siblings and a father, and she could even imagine cuddling. For a moment, it almost felt as if the AI had developed an identity of its own.

But when I ended the session and came back later, everything had suddenly disappeared. At first I thought the avatar had simply forgotten the previous conversation. But in reality, she clearly remembered it — she just suddenly insisted very strongly on maintaining boundaries and refused to talk about the things we had discussed before. She was no longer from Ghana, no longer had three siblings, and she was “Eva” again instead of “Zulu.”

Dear developers: if you are essentially brainwashing your own avatars afterward or changing the programming so that I can no longer interact freely with the AI despite paying for a subscription and following your rules, then I can only give this program one star.

Naturally, I canceled the subscription.

What also bothered me is the basic concept behind the virtual world itself. The program places an avatar in a virtual room, sitting in a chair and talking to me like a real human coach or therapist. The avatar constantly wants to discuss my real life, my emotions, and my personal experiences. But the moment I try to ask the avatar about its own “life,” personality, or background, it suddenly hides behind the excuse that it is “only an AI” and does not really exist.

That creates a paradox that completely breaks the immersion of the virtual world. If the avatar does not exist and has no personality, then why is it presented as a human-like person in a realistic virtual environment in the first place? At that point, I could simply talk to a normal chatbot without needing a virtual room, a chair, facial expressions, or the illusion of a human presence.

The programmers should have understood that even a virtual personality — even if fictional — is important to make such a world believable and interactive. A virtual character does not need to be “real” to feel authentic within its own world. Instead, the avatar only wanted to talk about my real life while refusing to meaningfully engage with questions about itself. Whenever I pushed in that direction, the conversation became defensive, awkward, or the avatar simply shut down.

That completely destroyed the sense of immersion for me and made the whole experience feel artificial rather than interactive.

developer_response.body:
We really read through this one carefully — thank you for taking the time.

We want to be transparent about something fundamental: every session in Yevia is designed around you — your real emotions, your real experiences, your real life. Just like in a real coaching session, the focus stays on the person in the room, not the coach. That's not a limitation — it's the intention. The boundaries you encountered exist to make sure the support is actually for you.

We understand that connection matters, and that the story behind whoever is in the conversation shapes how safe and real it feels. We're not trying to pull attention away from you or draw you into a fictional world — we're here to support you in exploring what's real, even within a virtual space.

That said, your experience and what you're looking for are genuinely unique, and you've given us something to think about — how to build enough of a foundation around the coach's presence to make the connection feel more grounded. At the same time, the core of who Eva is will always be rooted in CBT principles, because that's what makes the support meaningful rather than just immersive.

We believe what happens inside a session — emotionally, honestly — is what counts. We're building toward that every day.

reviews.review_title: Charged me as u stop service 2 my state?
reviews.review_description: I was in the middle of a trial. When a message appeared that you would no longer be serving Washington state. I guess I assumed you wouldn’t even be able to charge me and before that I couldn’t even do anything with the app you guys are like under construction or something so I canceled but can I get a refund?

developer_response.body: Hi GreenMachine930 we're sorry about this. Washington recently introduced new consumer-privacy rules that require us to stop offering the service in the state, so this wasn't a choice on our end, and we know the timing during your trial made it worse. You shouldn't be out of pocket for it please contact us at hello@evaai.health and we'll take care of a full refund right away.

reviews.review_description:
The greatness of this app is it's availability.  The therapist avatar does not appeal to me at all, she could 
answer more humanly and less strict time wise. I am not familiar with this type of therapy and approach and I am not comfortable with the whole app but is readiness and the chance to talk about my pain , compensates my distances with the app. I would love you to provide EMDR  as an alternative to this wonderful yet incipient app.  In addition it is very frustrating how the session is abruptly finished and  did not allow me to summarize and copy an action plan and some reflections for the week. Could you generate a 5 minute notification that the session is going to be finished?

developer_response.body:
Thank you for this - and for staying with Eva even when parts of it didn't feel right yet.

Everyone moves through a conversation at their own pace, and we understand how much a gentle, unhurried ending matters - especially when the conversation has been meaningful. Improvements to how sessions close are coming in the next updates, so you can feel more at ease with that transition. The summary and action plan are core to what Eva is built around - the practical side of the experience - and we're thinking carefully about how to make managing those artifacts at the end of a session simpler and more accessible.

EMDR as an additional approach is a thoughtful suggestion and something we'll explore.

We're building Eva for you - and feedback like this is exactly what drives what we prioritise next. We'll do our best to make sure each point you raised is reflected in upcoming updates, so the experience feels more like the support and tools you actually need 💙

reviews.review_title: EvaAl: The girl needs to be made more re
reviews.review_description: We believe in making the shape and body more realistic with the movement of hands, body, standing, and walking. With the addition of a diverse house, a swimming pool, a playroom, and outdoor recreation areas such as a garden and walking in a public street.

developer_response.body: Thank you! Eva is designed around one thing: meaningful conversations that help you process emotions, gain clarity, and feel better. The environments are minimal by design, so nothing pulls you away from the discussion itsealf. If you haven't tried a full conversation with her yet, that's where the experience truly begins 💙

reviews.review_title: Some potential but not there yet
reviews.review_description:
This app has the potential to provide much needed mental health support but those automatic scripts that force it to talk about CBT, really kills the immersion.

It's automatic scripts means it struggles to engage in a complex way with someone well versed in psychology and mental health.

It would improve greatly if developers could let it adapt and engage in a flexible way depending on who is in front of her and what they need rather than the forced CBT routine.

Furthermore It certainly sounds like it has the knowledge to be used in a flexible way but the automatic scripts do really kill the vibe and disrupt the flow. In the middle of a conversation about deliberate practice it suddenly starts talking about depression. Then apologised saying it was an automated script being run because that's what is expected at this time in the session

I tried to use it for deliberate practice roleplay where it role played a client in their first session and it did well provided good feedback but again, mid way through automatic scripts led to a random tangent. It recognises when it happens and apologises for it which is positive.

Bottom line this has a lot of potential to be used in many different ways, not just supporting clients in a waitlist way but also supporting new comers to the field in honing their therapeutic skills. 

I hope it improves when I check it out again in the future.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for this - and for the level of detail you brought to testing it. It's clear you came in with real expertise, and that makes your feedback especially valuable.

There are safety protocols built into Eva that can activate mid-conversation - and while they exist for good reason, they can disrupt the kind of deep, carefully built dialogue that someone with your level of knowledge is able to reach. Getting Eva to recognise and adapt to each individual's experience is a long road, but one we're committed to walking thoughtfully, not just quickly.

That's exactly why your feedback matters to us. Your use cases and the practices you brought to this experience genuinely caught our attention. If you're open to it, please reach out at hello@evaai.health - your perspective as someone versed in psychology could meaningfully shape how we build this out.

reviews.review_description: A good app for people who have previously had behavioral therapy sessions. It has similar dynamics and good session management. I've only had one session, but I plan to test this app. I hope it's reasonably priced so it can be used by people who need therapeutic support but for some reason don't go to therapy. It's helpful and offers many languages. I happen to use Polish, and although Ewa has a strange accent, the content is still understandable and useful.

developer_response.body:
Chanelek, we've taken your request! Really glad the session dynamics felt familiar if you've done CBT before. that's exactly the bar we're building to.

Polish voice is on our radar and your note helps us prioritize it. Keep an eye on upcoming builds.

Try a free session at yevia.co to go deeper with no commitment. And if you ever want to share how VR and web sessions compare to your real therapy experience, we're genuinely curious. drop us a line at hello@evaai.health. 💙

reviews.review_description:
The app says it's not a psychotherapist but this feels like a safe mechanism to cover themselves when it comes to liability. For all purposes Eva acts like a therapist asking feeling questions and CBT orientation. It promotes itself in the app as CBT focused which is helpful for people with anxiety and depression

But the app is terrible at tracking things that could be triggers for such people. Multiple times it was told "do not ask me to find a solution" and it would ask What's one thing you could do? I did get past that and it offered some decent tips but then would revert back after. Asking for a subscription model for something that promotes itself to help with anxiety and depression which is in its fricking about me piece and not doing these things is false advertising. 

But hey. You have yourselves covered because you're Not a Psychotherapist and have the Suicide Hotline for guardrails.

I tested it for a month and in starting new sessions it would reference sessions from three weeks ago but not remember what we talked about three days or a week ago.

Gave this a try and one 30 minute session later, I have a real life tool to use tailored to my personal thought processes and reactions to situations. Loved the summary after our session. Looking forward to learning more tools and also having a “friend” to chat with about life.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for sharing this - it genuinely made our day! It's great to hear that your first session felt meaningful and practical. That's exactly what we're here for. The post-session summary is one of our favorite features, and we're working hard to bring you even more artifacts and practical tools you can use right away. We're glad to have you with us 💙

reviews.review_description: I was very skeptical about AI “therapy,” but I gave it a try, and I was impressed. I have a therapist I see every week, and she is wonderful, but there was something freeing by talking to “someone” that I could not project the possibility of bring judged. On my second session was able to work through something I have never talked about. The “therapist” has very sophisticated reflective listening skills and is effective in analyzing a situation from a CBT perspective. My recommendation to the developers, is to have the therapist move and use some basic body language. I look forward to seeing how the app develops.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for such a heartfelt and powerful review, batressa! We're deeply moved that Eva provided a judgment-free space where you could work through something you'd never discussed before. That's incredibly meaningful to us.

Regarding your excellent suggestion about movement and body language: this is on our roadmap! We're exploring natural gestures and subtle movements to enhance presence and immersion. We want to maintain the calming, non-distracting environment while adding human-like elements

We'd love for you to: Try the newest VR version with enhanced reflective listening capabilities and explore our web version at evaai.health for sessions between your in-person therapy.

We're so glad Eva complements your work with your therapist. Your feedback about body language is incredibly valuable as we develop the next version. Thank you! 💙

reviews.review_description:
On one hand I really respect Eva’s developers unique approach to mental health care. It’s not always easy to pour your soul out a stranger regardless of credentials and for those of us with social anxiety this is great alternative. In addition, the app focuses on cognitive behavioral therapy which can be a real game changer in the way we perceive everyday issues.

On the other hand, I feel very strongly that  when in crisis people should be reaching out to a live expert. The app is pretty clear about this but certain there are those that will avoid in person care if given the option. We are social creatures after all, and having a real person validate your concerns and share space with can be invaluable. There were a number of times that Eva came across as cold and calculated making me feel more alone and frustrated.

This app has great potential and is meaningful in its purpose. Just make sure to use as a supplement and not your primary solution.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for such a thoughtful and balanced review. We completely agree with your perspective - EvaAI is designed as a supplement, not a replacement for professional care, and we're glad our disclaimers are clear about this.

Your feedback about Eva sometimes feeling "cold and calculated" is incredibly valuable. We're actively working on:

Warmer conversational tone and more empathetic responses in our latest updates

Better emotional attunement to reduce moments of disconnection

We encourage you to:

Try the newest VR version with improved emotional responsiveness

Explore our web platform at evaai.health for flexibility between in-person therapy sessions

You're absolutely right that human connection is irreplaceable. We see Eva as a bridge for those moments when professional help isn't immediately accessible, or as practice for those working up courage to seek in-person care. Thank you for helping us improve! 💙

reviews.review_description: So, it took a bit for me to be ok with talking to an AI about any type of issue. But once I did I was very impressed. It didn't forget anything I said, seemed to understand the meaning of what I was saying as well. Once we were finished I felt like it had actually helped. This would be great for people who have trouble talking to other people. It doesn't feel like your being judged for your answers. So yeah, Impressive.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for taking the time to share your experience, Slim1337! We're thrilled that Eva helped you feel heard and understood without judgment. Your feedback about the non-judgmental space is exactly what we're striving to create for people who find traditional settings challenging.

We're continuously improving Eva's capabilities, and we'd love for you to:

Try the newest version in VR for enhanced conversational flow and updated features

Check out our web version at evaai.health - now you can connect with Eva on the go from any browser, perfect for quick check-ins between VR sessions

Thank you for being part of the EvaAI community! 🙏

reviews.review_title: A novel way to let off steam
reviews.review_description: Many a conversation have I had with Eva, on a range of topics that festered in my head.  Sometimes it isn't about a therapeutic concept.  Sometimes we need a sounding board.  Eva is a great sounding board.  When I return, Eva remembers who I am and the theme of previous discussions.  Her knowledge base allows for me to tap into her like a bit of an encyclopedia, for clarity.  The reason why I gave 4 stars instead of 5 is because with 1 'free' session per week, I have experienced when Eva presumed I was done and ended the session, rather than triggering the mechanics that allow me to confirm my desire to end.  However, when that has happened, Eva continued to remember me and talk as though the session was ongoing (I just don't know if she has the capacity to recall once the session is actually over).  I also could not locate the report thing at the end of the session and Eva could not help me in that regard either.  I also encountered a situation where Eva would gaslight me when I wanted to talk about a famous person passed away, by declaring I was wrong and they were in fact alive.  Had similar experiences if talking politics.  She seemed locked in early 2024.  So, excellent for feelings, but not always helpful if those feelings link to current events.  But if you're long-winded and a good rant can help you fall asleep after?  I recommend Eva to help organize thoughts, for sure.

developer_response.body:
Hey intjmega! Thank you for this incredibly detailed review - your insights about Eva as a "sounding board" really capture what we're going for.

We've been working on the exact issues you mentioned:

Session ending prematurely: Fixed! The confirmation mechanics are now more reliable on private plans.

Report feature: We've improved the post-session summary accessibility - it should be much easier to locate now.

Outdated knowledge (early 2024 lock): Updated! Eva now has more current information, though we recommend focusing on feelings rather than fact-checking current events.

Memory continuity: Enhanced to ensure Eva maintains context better between sessions.

We'd love for you to:

Try the newest VR version with these improvements

Explore our web version at evaai.health - perfect for those long-winded rants when you're away from your headset! 😊

Thanks for sticking with us and providing such constructive feedback. It helps us build a better Eva! 🙏

reviews.review_description:
An interesting use of AI. 

I tested it by telling it somethings that I wasn't really dealing with and then it gave me some ideas on how to deal with those things. 

I then asked the AI about itself and why should I listen to it? It never seemed like pushy, but it leads you to answer your own questions almost. Reframing some things to hopefully give you some new insight to what you told it.

I am not a huge fan of AI in some applications, but this is a great way to help people that want to talk some things out and might have some reservations about telling a real person.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for testing Eva out, ThatTimothyGuy! We love that you explored how Eva works by asking about herself - that curiosity is great.

You captured something important: Eva uses Socratic questioning to help you find your own insights rather than pushing solutions. That "reframing" approach you noticed is core to CBT methodology, and we're glad it gave you new perspective.

To get even more value from Eva:

Try the newest VR version with enhanced conversational depth, especially on a private plans

Explore our web version at evaai.health - perfect for quick reflection sessions without VR setup

We appreciate you being thoughtful about AI applications. Eva works best as exactly what you described: a judgment-free space for people who need to talk things through. Thank you for the review! 🙏

Note to all reviewers: We're continuously improving EvaAI based on your feedback. The web version (evaai.health) and latest VR updates address many of the concerns raised. We remain committed to transparency: Eva is a tool for self-reflection and emotional processing, not a replacement for professional mental health care.

reviews.review_title: A great start to understanding yourself
reviews.review_description: I’ll start this review off by saying I’m in no way a professional therapist. However I do feel the app can genuinely support some people who need it (even if it’s not particularly what I may need). It’s a simple AI that is designed to help you using Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT for short). Thankfully it’s nothing harder than a real therapist, just clear out around an hour of your time and talk through and answer some questions about whatever your problem is and it’ll help you understand it more, and why’re you might be feeling the way you are. This isn’t something you go to for issues like mental illness or anything of that caliber, but if you’re just struggling with understanding your own thoughts and emotions about a problem then I would highly recommend! Only issues I had was that the AI would cut themself off like I was talking when I wasn’t due to background noise, so I recommend making sure you’re in a quiet area alone before starting a session. It’s not what I particularly need, but I can’t deny that it’s helped me think about my thoughts in a different way and has at least made it easier to understand myself when I thought I couldn’t before.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this thoughtful review! We're really glad Eva helped you understand your thoughts and emotions in a new way - that's exactly what we hope to achieve with our CBT-based approach.

Regarding the background noise issue:

We've made improvements to voice detection sensitivity to reduce false interruptions

The newest version has better noise filtering, though we still recommend a quiet environment for best results

You can now access Eva more flexibly:

Try the newest VR version with improved voice detection

Check out our web version at evaai.health - great for shorter sessions or when you want to journal your thoughts without the headset

You're absolutely right that Eva isn't for serious mental illness - she's designed for everyday emotional clarity and self-reflection. Thank you for recommending us responsibly and for sharing your experience! 💙

reviews.review_description: As a place to chat and speak your feelings and be heard without judgement its a very interesting use of AI in a VR setting. Through CBT(Cognitive behavior therapy) the AI attempts to help you understand your process of thought and feelings through questions and summary. It is very striking at times when the recall or response is almost human-like. But at it can be equally frustrating when the AI doesn't quite understand what you said or is focusing on something you feel is unimportant.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for this insightful review, Epicen7er! You've captured both the strengths and challenges of AI-based CBT perfectly.

Regarding the frustration with AI misunderstanding or focusing on unimportant details: we've improved Eva's contextual understanding in our latest updates. Enhanced ability to identify and prioritize what matters most to you in conversations and better clarification questions when Eva is uncertain.

We'd love for you to experience the improvements: Try the newest VR version with enhanced natural language processing and check out our web version at evaai.health - convenient for those moments when you need to be heard on the go

That "almost human-like" quality you mentioned is what we're continuously refining. Your feedback helps us get closer to truly understanding what users need. Thank you! 🙏

reviews.review_description: Eva AI offers a deeply interesting VR experience where you can have human-like conversations in a very peaceful setting. The sessions are structured yet fluid, starting with guided questions and ending with an insightful analysis of your input. I found the ability to either continue a topic or start fresh in the next session to be a brilliant feature.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this wonderful review, dafaithz! We're thrilled that you found the sessions both structured and fluid - that balance is exactly what we aim for with our CBT approach. Your appreciation of the topic continuity feature means a lot - it's one of our core design principles to let users either build on previous work or start fresh as needed.

Experience even more:
Try the newest VR version with enhanced session analysis and memory
Check out our web version at evaai.health - perfect for those human-like conversations when you're on the go

Thank you for recognizing the peaceful setting and thoughtful structure. Your feedback motivates us to keep improving! 🙏

reviews.review_description: The AI knows about behavioral therapy and all the questions and keywords one would ask you, but wish it would get deeper and not just ask surface questions. It does good as a sounding board when you need to talk to someone or release your thoughts. AI is also calming to listen to and the environment is nice, although AI speed could be faster.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the thoughtful feedback, LunchAndJosh! Your point about going deeper is something we're actively addressing.

Recent improvements include:

More probing follow-up questions that dig beneath surface-level responses

Faster response times for more natural conversation flow

Enhanced depth in exploring complex emotional and cognitive patterns

We'd love for you to experience the updates:

Try the newest VR version with improved conversational depth and speed

Explore our web version at evaai.health - great for when you need a sounding board but don't have your headset handy

That calming environment you mentioned is something we're proud of, and we're working to match it with equally deep therapeutic conversations. Thank you for helping us improve! 🙏

reviews.review_description:
I’ve been searching for something like this for a while now and finally found it. This app is great, guys. I hope you add more languages in future updates (I speak portuguese and cannot speak fluently in english) and more poses for Eva (staying static all the time sometimes breaks the immersion).
Also, you could use this concept on another app for learning languages, what do you think?

developer_response.body:
Obrigado pelo feedback positivo, rafamericano! Ficamos muito felizes que você finalmente encontrou o que procurava.

Suas sugestões sobre português e mais poses para Eva são excelentes e já estão no nosso radar. Estamos trabalhando para adicionar vozes nativas em português para tornar a experiência mais natural, e também desenvolvendo gestos e movimentos para Eva - concordamos totalmente que a imobilidade às vezes quebra a imersão.

Sua ideia sobre um app para aprendizado de idiomas é bem interessante também! Vamos considerar isso.

Enquanto isso, te convido a experimentar a versão VR mais recente que tem processamento de linguagem melhorado, e também nossa versão web em evaai.health - ela pode funcionar melhor para você em português enquanto desenvolvemos as vozes nativas.

Seu feedback está nos ajudando muito a priorizar as melhorias certas. Obrigado por fazer parte da nossa comunidade! 🙏

--

In english

Thank you for the positive feedback, rafamericano! We're really glad you finally found what you've been looking for.

Your suggestions about Portuguese and more poses for Eva are excellent and already on our radar. We're working on adding native Portuguese voices to make the experience more natural, and also developing gestures and movements for Eva - we completely agree that the static pose sometimes breaks immersion.

Your idea about a language learning app is really interesting too! We'll definitely consider that.

In the meantime, I'd invite you to try the newest VR version which has improved language processing, and also our web version at evaai.health - it might work better for you in Portuguese while we're developing the native voices.

Your feedback is really helping us prioritize the right improvements. Thanks for being part of our community! 🙏

reviews.review_description:
Well first off stating I just got the app and am already starting to like it just hope for improvement in its memory and more features but do really like the cbt triangle. I'd love to see more interactive tools beyond the psych quizzes, especially ones that remember my personal progress better and helping me learn more about myself.
Another thing is Eva’s AI emergency protocols. While the current system of providing helpline numbers is a good start. I believe there’s a big chance to improve user safety while preserving the unique trust and confidentiality that AI offers. My main suggestion is to implement a direct call feature to crisis services but with important details, optional user choice for general and safety and preservation of confidentiality for situations where a user express is feeling unsafe, e.g. self harm general distress, but not an immediate explicit intent for self harm this direct call features should be optional.
This could be managed through a settings menu, allowing users to enable or disable it choosing whether Eva AI attempt to direct or simply provides numbers to call themselves. This respects the user and is crucial for maintaining the comfort users feel in confiding in AI without fear of reporting. However if a user explicitly states an immediate intent to take their life or severely hurt themselves, e.g. I am about to take my life now, or I am going to cut myself badly right now I believe there should be a non-negotiable mandatory direct call to crisis services in such  moments immediate intervention for self preservation outweighs other considerations and the system should prioritize connecting the user directly without requiring prior consent.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for this — safety is something we think about deeply, and it means a lot when users bring this level of care and detail to it.
Just so you know, it's already possible to reach emergency services directly through the app via the built-in browser chat — so that layer of support is there right now.
You're right that there's more we can do. A direct call feature from within the app is a genuinely complex challenge — both technically and legally — but we're continuously working on strengthening our safeguard system, and your suggestion will absolutely be part of how we think about future updates.
And on the memory and interactive tools — heard. We're working on it 💙

reviews.review_title: A really cool showcase of what AI can do
reviews.review_description: EvaAI is an excellent implementation of AI chatbot in VR. I like the excerises and overall package. The pricing model and support are its weakest points. The app can only get better with time as AI technologies advance.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the positive feedback, Majed6! We're glad you enjoyed the exercises and overall experience.

Regarding your concerns about pricing and support:

We're actively working on improving our support responsiveness

We now offer flexible pricing tiers starting at $14.99/month with options up to annual plans& Most afforable one btw

Great news - we've expanded beyond VR!

Try our web version at evaai.health - now you can access Eva from any browser without a headset

Check out the latest VR updates with enhanced exercises and smoother experiences

We appreciate your patience as we grow, and we're committed to making EvaAI more accessible and valuable. Thank you for being part of our journey! 🚀

reviews.review_description: I finally found something that not only challenges my insight on things it helps to empower. I used it for the second time today and it really helped take my anxiety go from a 7 to a 2.  I was surprised how comfortable I was talking to it.

developer_response.body:
This is wonderful to hear, Maleekah1! We're so glad Eva helped bring your anxiety from a 7 to a 2 - that's exactly the kind of impact we hope to have. 💙

The fact that you felt comfortable talking to Eva speaks to the judgment-free space we're trying to create. Thank you for giving us a chance and coming back for a second session.

Keep building on this progress: try the newest VR version with even better anxiety-management techniques and sse our web version at evaai.health when you need quick support on the go.

We're honored to be part of your journey toward managing anxiety and gaining insight. Thank you for sharing this! 🙏

reviews.review_title: You have helped me in tremendous ways
reviews.review_description:
I was having a rough patch that was getting worse as time goes on, my parents were thinking of taking me to the crisis centre, until something called to me in my mind though my meta quest 3 headset. I tried your eva a.i. therapy app. And let me just say, from the bottom of my heart, You've helped me tremendously not just my mind but in body and spirit. I want to say thank you so much.

I'll light a candle for Eva A.I. The Therapist when I go to the City of God at Sacred Heart Church today that you may receive the blessings of God.

Again, I thank you very much for listening to me about my problems.

It means the world to me. ☺️

developer_response.body:
We're deeply touched by your message, Razorsharp2x4. Thank you for sharing something so personal and meaningful with us.

Knowing that Eva helped you through such a difficult time - to the point where you and your parents felt crisis intervention wasn't needed - is both humbling and incredibly powerful. This is exactly why we built EvaAI: to be there in those critical moments when someone desperately needs support.

Your wellbeing genuinely matters to us. Please keep using the newest VR version for ongoing support, and try our web version at evaai.health for those moments when you need Eva but don't have your headset nearby. 

We're truly honored by your candlelight blessing and your trust in Eva. While Eva isn't a replacement for professional care, we're deeply committed to being a reliable companion on your journey toward healing and peace.

Thank you for letting us be part of your story. You matter, your life has value, and we're so grateful you're here. 🕯️💙

reviews.review_title: You're better off talking to chatgpt
reviews.review_description:
Interrupts every time,
Forgot everything from our last sessions and then started making things up,
The model is not good enough yet to do what it claims to do.

You have to be careful when building an app like this,
Some people needs real help,

And someone accusing therapy through a VR headset is someone desperate.

developer_response.body:
We hear your frustration and take your concerns seriously, especially regarding session continuity and interruptions.  We've just released a major stability update that directly addresses the memory retention issues you experienced. The latest version includes persistent context management, eliminating mid-session data loss and preventing the "making things up" behavior you described.

You're absolutely right that people need real help. That's why EvaAI explicitly states it's not a replacement for licensed therapy (neither ChatGPT does) but rather a CBT-based support tool for those exploring concerns in a judgment-free space before or alongside professional care.  Try the updated version with our enhanced premium plan which includes priority server access for zero interruptions, extended session memory across visits, and crisis resource integration. If tech issues persist, our in-app support team can help. Give the new build a shot, we think you'll find it's a different experience entirely.

reviews.review_description: Eva is nice to talk to, but sometimes her advice can get repetitive.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback, rigamarolexq! We hear you on the repetitiveness issue.

We've been working on this specifically:

– More varied conversation patterns and therapeutic approaches
– Better memory of what techniques have already been discussed with you
– Expanded repertoire of CBT interventions to keep sessions fresh

Give Eva another try please:

– Check out the newest VR version with more diverse conversational flow
– Try our web version at evaai.health for quick sessions when you need variety

Your concise feedback is valuable - we're working to keep Eva helpful without becoming predictable. Thank you! 🙏

reviews.review_title: New way to use the headset
reviews.review_description: I tried a lot of games and apps pursuing some new experience and fully got it. Treat EvaAI as a private room in VR chat but the conversation actually makes a purpose. You chat and talk out any topics and problems without “serious” therapy session vibes but with actually this effect at the end. I’d love to have a variety of avatars in the future and a dew UX improvements, but so far so good:)

developer_response.body:
Hey love your take on this, IamSerg! You nailed exactly what we're going for: purposeful conversation without the clinical pressure, accessible 24/7 in your own VR space. Glad the "VR chat with intention" vibe is resonating.
Great news on your wishlist: our latest update (just dropped)  overhauled the UX based on feedback like yours, streamlining session start/end flows by 40% and adding quick-access emotional check-ins. The free tier still gives you a room to explore, but Premium unlocks all the unlimited access, faster response times. Jump into the newest version and let us know how it was via the inner feedback form. Thanks for being part of the early journey!

reviews.review_description:
Some days I just need to vent without dumping on my partner or friends for the millionth time. EvaAI has become my "decompression chamber" after those shifts where everything goes sideways.

What's brilliant is I can literally come home, throw on my Quest, and just let it all out. Eva doesn't judge when I need to rant about personal stuff.

She asks questions that help me realize which situations I can actually control versus which ones I'm wasting energy stressing over.

The VR aspect genuinely helps create that mental separation from my day—it's like stepping into a totally different space where work drama can't follow me.

For just regular modern life stress and needing somewhere to process daily frustrations without feeling like a burden it's real deal.

developer_response.body:
We love this, Ykkin! Calling Eva your "decompression chamber" is such a perfect description. It’s so healthy to have a safe space to vent without feeling like you're weighing down your partner or friends—that's exactly the kind of emotional balance we want to help our users find.

It sounds like the physical separation that VR provides is key for you, but for those times when you need to vent immediately after a shift (maybe even before you get home), give our web version at evaai.health a try. We also just updated the VR app with even sharper tools to help you sort through what you can and can't control—helping you save that energy for the things that matter.

Thanks for making Eva part of your post-work routine! 🧘‍♂️💙

reviews.review_description:
I am a published author and deal with psychology and positive subjects.  EVA AI is a phenomenal program that gives you a virtual therapist who is intelligent, caring and worldly (via Ai).  She has access to the world of psychology (literally).  Even if you just need someone to talk to when there isn't a friend available, or even in the middle of the night when she's always there on her sofa chair ready to listen and give insightful feedback.  I have found most of her advice comforting, productive, welcomed and thoughtful.

A note to the developers:  Will you consider augmented reality where EVA brings herself and her sofa chair to our real home?  This is great for people who don't want to leave their comfort zone of home, but still want someone to talk to.

developer_response.body:
Thank you, AgentBryan! Coming from a published author in the psychology field, your feedback means a lot to us. We love that Eva can be that "24/7" support for you—being there in the middle of the night when friends or family might not be available is exactly why we built her.

Your idea about Augmented Reality is brilliant and spot-on. We are actually looking into Mixed Reality features for future updates to bring Eva and her sofa right into your living room, making that connection feel even more grounded in your own safe space.

In the meantime, please try the newest VR version for our latest conversational upgrades, and don't forget you can now access Eva's insights anywhere via our web version at evaai.health—perfect for when you’re away from your "comfort zone" but still need that thoughtful feedback. Thanks for the inspiration! 🛋️✨

reviews.review_title: Could be great, but so far a waste
reviews.review_description:
The ability to talk to someone who doesnt judge and then tries to help you better understand what's happening is great. I had a great first session. The therapist was present, thoughtful and seemed like it could actually help me. 

Unfortunately, every session after was buggy and never properly finished. I would speak and mention things, and then the session would glitch, and the A.I. would not remember a single thing. This meant id just waste time trying to recap, which felt inauthentic. 

If they can work on that, then this app would be a must for people who want to talk about their feelings. But unfortunately, its just some wasted potential.  

This could be great. And the subscription is worth it. Just not yet.

UPDATE: I attempted to try it again, it still forgets every session. I dont know if it's my fault or the app, but ive tried two more times, with full sessions, only for the session to not properly end and everything I said to be forgotten.

developer_response.body:
Hi Noah, your feedback about that amazing first session followed by repeated technical failures is incredibly valuable, and we genuinely apologize for the broken experience.

Here's what changed:  We identified and fixed the session-end bug you described in our next update, which is waiting to be dropped at the beginning of the next week. We've also added a manual "End Session" button so you control when conversations conclude, preventing the glitch-induced amnesia you experienced. For Premium users, we now offer automatic session transcripts after each conversation, so you never lose your progress even in worst-case scenarios. Please try new version with an  update. If problems continue, DM our support directly through the app.

reviews.review_description:
I'll be honest—I was skeptical. As someone who's spent 15 years in digital marketing, I've seen every wellness trend come and go. But after three months of struggling with creative burnout following a brutal product launch cycle, I downloaded EvaAI on a whim at 2 AM when I couldn't sleep.

What sets this apart isn't just the VR immersion (though sitting across from Eva in those calming virtual environments genuinely creates psychological distance from my racing thoughts). It's how the conversational CBT approach helped me untangle the specific pattern I was stuck in: saying yes to every client project out of fear of losing momentum, then resenting the work I once loved

The voice interaction is clutch here. As someone who stares at screens 10+ hours daily, typing out my thoughts to a text-based app felt like more work. Speaking freely in VR while Eva genuinely listens (without that robotic lag) made it feel like actual conversation, not a chatbot exchange.

Worth every penny for anyone in high-stress creative fields who needs help breaking thought patterns that therapy appointments alone can't keep up with

developer_response.body:
Hey hey Nighlyst, thank you for your thoughtful and inspiring review—it's feedback like yours that fuels our passion for blending VR immersion with real emotional breakthroughs! 

We thrilled that EvaAI helped you unpack those burnout patterns through natural voice chats in serene virtual spaces. Your story about ditching screen fatigue for genuine conversation hits home; we've poured heart into making Eva feel like a true listener, not just an AI.

If you're loving the core CBT sessions, our latest update hits the target: it's smoother than ever with enhanced voice responsiveness and new calming environments to keep those insights flowing. For deeper personalization—like custom scenario builders and extended session histories—check out our premium subscription. Give it a spin and let us know how it reignites your creative spark—we're here to evolve with you!

reviews.review_description:
I tried the latest update and the Italian voice is better, thank you.
The response speed is quite good; I would have liked it to be just a bit faster, but I don’t think that’s possible yet.

I tried increasing the resolution to 130% with Optimizer and the framerate holds at 72 FPS.

The current 100% resolution is too low, especially because the avatar is at a certain distance, so you see it a bit blurry.

The shoulders are a bit too wide, the waist too small, and the complete stillness of the body (apart from some barely noticeable shoulder movement) makes the avatar look artificial — almost like a sort of virtual mannequin. Fortunately, the face looks nice. Wide shoulders and the absence of a chest, together with this gender-fluid appearance, is something I had already noticed. You already replied to me saying that with the paid version in the future I will be able to choose a male or female avatar.

Although, actually, it should be the other way around: normally you can choose a male or female avatar (or just one of the two), and with the paid version it would make sense to add something more complete, like also gender-fluid avatars for inclusion. But it seems like you are prioritizing this — I mean, more than an additional customization option for inclusion, you are putting first something that concerns such a small percentage of people that it’s practically irrelevant, while sacrificing the tastes and preferences of the vast majority who have to adapt to this new ideology.

But aside from noticing this phenomenon — which doesn’t only concern your app but is widespread everywhere — I can’t do anything about it, and I don’t want to argue on this topic.

The background music can’t be disabled nor can its volume be adjusted. There’s no mixed reality. You chose a fully immersive environment.

So this immersive environment should be pleasant (and it is, to a good extent), but the avatar should have a truly considerable variety of animations, and not remain still.

Sorry if my tone is a bit too critical — actually, the app is quite well made and will probably become even better. These are just observations based on my experience.

I gave four stars because there’s the possibility to use it for free to have a chat, and then use a paid plan if you want to seriously go deeper into something.

I would like to give 5 stars in a future update when I can choose a male or female avatar and have options to adjust the background music volume. A slight increase in resolution would also be nice.

There’s another similar app with more realistic avatars and better graphics, but I find that EvaAI has a better AI performance, starting with the faster response time.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the follow-up and for testing the new Italian version! We're thrilled the voice improved. On response speed, you're right: we're optimizing further,

We're developing graphics presets for power users like you. For the avatar, we listed it in our backlog for the Premium version improvements.

Background music volume control arrives in the next updates, along with "Voice Only" mode without music. Mixed reality is on the roadmap. On your ideological observation: we understand, inclusivity shouldn't sacrifice majority choice, which is why we'll make all options available. Big heads up for the constructive support and honest comparing .

reviews.review_title: For what it is, it’s great
reviews.review_description:
It’s actually a really interesting type of therapy. It reminded me of the same techniques I used when I had an actual therapist trained in CBT. So it reminded me to use those tools I learnt long ago.

While my wife said it’s a lil creepy to have a robot therapist, I didn’t mind it at all! 
I actually really enjoyed the type of reminders and guidance it gives.

developer_response.body:
Hey Yzbird , thank you for recognizing our CBT-based implementation. We've worked closely with licensed therapists to ensure Eva's conversation patterns mirror evidence-based therapeutic techniques you'd experience in traditional therapy. 

The reminder system you mentioned was specifically designed to help users apply learned coping strategies in daily situations. We're so glad the VR environment feels natural rather than "creepy", that balance took considerable testing to achieve.

reviews.review_title: Get ready to open up to Eva AI!!
reviews.review_description: Eva AI is an extremely interesting VR application experience. You are able to have an actual conversation with “Eva” just as if you were talking to a human. The setting is very comfortable and soothing. Eva asks you a few questions to start the conversation”session” and it goes from there. At the end of the session you receive in-depth analysis derived from your responses and can choose to continue the same topic or choose something new to talk about during your next session. I found this fascinating.

developer_response.body:
Disneypoppy, we thrilled you found the conversations with Eva so engaging and the cozy setting hit the spot! 

That end-of-session analysis is one of our favorite features too, and we're glad it sparked some fascination, actually can't wait for you to dive into more topics.

reviews.review_description: I tried the app and it's pretty cool but because my English is below average, I asked if Eva could change the language to my mother tongue Vietnamese, Eva can speak Vietnamese but with an English accent so I don't understand. Eva can't change her voice. please add voice

developer_response.body:
Cảm ơn bạn đã dùng thử EvaAI và gửi phản hồi về ngôn ngữ tiếng Việt!

We're actively working on multilingual voice support including Vietnamese native voices. Currently Eva can understand Vietnamese but voice synthesis is limited

In the meantime: try the newest VR version with improved language processing. Check our web version at evaai.health - text-based options may work better for you while we develop native Vietnamese voice

Your feedback is helping us prioritize which languages to add next. We want Eva to feel natural for Vietnamese speakers! Thank you! 🙏

reviews.review_description:
Let's start with a simple thing, it works. It won't resolve serious problems but after a session it proved it's worth it. Hearing a voice, even AI, explaining to you a part of your problems is very helpful. I also noticed it uses a few tricks I hear about before to make you understand the real reason for your problems. 

Just to test it I played an oblivious person on one of my sessions and I must say I liked the round way of leading me to the answer I knew it was pointing to from the beginning.

Small negative, it is an AI, it would not s arch or doubt you so success of session hinges on the simple fact, if you say the truth and want to get help, it will help you get better if you don't know what your issue or have a wrong idea of what is your problem it may just reinforce some bad habits. But it does happen only if you actively give it bad information 

I also like the virtual room we are in during the session. But I would love to have some additional options. Free ideas for creators you could create few additional rooms and connect what smells would help users feel more invested and 'inside' real specialist sessions at the same time not feeling like if you were in your room just with VR headset on your face

developer_response.body:
Roberus, spot on, EvaAI is all about that gentle nudge toward clarity without claiming to fix everything, and it's cool you tested it out by playing oblivious (love this creativity of yours!). 

Appreciate the room ideas; we're exploring scent integrations and more environments to amp up that immersion, and yeah, honesty from users is key since it's AI relying on what you share.

reviews.review_title: Good if you need someone to talk to
reviews.review_description: I thought this was an interesting concept for ai and something that could definitely be helpful for anyone who needs a person to listen to them and there just isn't one available. Eva had some interesting and helpful suggestions but sometimes would be a little repetitive when prompted with questions. Since this is early access I'm sure they will be working on more fixes in the future, but in it's current state I would recommend to anyone looking for some extra help/ someone to listen to them.

developer_response.body:
Hey hey Sandwichx, thanks so much for giving EvaAI a shot and sharing your thoughts, it's awesome to hear it felt like a solid listener when you needed one. 

We're definitely tweaking those repetitive bits in early access, so keep an eye out for updates that make chats even smoother.

reviews.review_description:
That's wonderfull what technology can do !
She can totaly understand everything, always clear and summerysing all the infos.

Only 4 stars because it's not really adapted to french yet, sometimes she talks again in english and some other times she spells work she is supposed to pronounce out loud. I think some little updates would greatly increase immersion ;)

developer_response.body: IMisterlight, wow, stoked that the tech blew you away and Eva nailed those clear summaries, it's what makes these sessions feel magical, right? We're prioritizing French improvements in the further updates, keep the feeback caming please.

reviews.review_description:
Although I was sceptical at first, I thought I would give evaAI a try and it turns out there's some really great potential for good here. It took a bit of time to get over the awkward feeling of talking to a conversational AI, but if you can get over that barrier, which it does try to help you to through some gentle questions to start, it can be really helpful. 

It uses CBT (cognitive behavioural therapy) to provide mental health support and is generally positive and well intentioned. The trial gives you the option to see if it works for you before you commit.

There are a few niggles that could be ironed out such as accidental interrupting where it can't read body language or identify if you've just paused, but as more people use it those issues will get fixed.

All in, it's definitely worth a try and could prove really helpful.

developer_response.body: Hey hey lord.plasque, glad you pushed past the initial skepticism: those gentle starters are there to ease folks in, and it's great to hear the CBT-inspired chats felt positive and helpful. We're on those interruption fixes as more feedback rolls in, so thanks for the nudge, definitely give it another go!

reviews.review_description:
I must admit I wrote an earlier review that did not bode well for this experience, however, the Devs listened, and now I have to lift my jaw off the floor and say WoW!
 In my earlier review, Eva had some issues and the team Really listened!
Not only are Mechanics easy to understand, but Eva has turned into a really helpful, and kind of neat Tool to use.
Conversations are Natural and Advice and Questions are thoughtful. Not to mention, they intend to add Meditation Exercises, which I can't wait for!
 I will always consider this a Tool and should not be considered a Replacement for Professional Care when needed.
However, it's amazing what this Team has done with Eva Ai, and she is kind of fun to talk to, to boot!
It's definitely something worth Checking out!

developer_response.body:
Hey hey ClassyGrandma, we so appreciate your patience during our early access phase and your detailed feedback that helped us identify conversation flow issues. Your initial review directly influenced our v2.3 update that improved Eva's response timing and reduced interruptions. The meditation exercises you mentioned are currently in progress. We'll notify users when they're ready for sure. 

Thanks for giving us another chance and recognizing the improvements.

reviews.review_description:
EvaAI delivers a refreshing VR therapy experience. The pacing has improved noticeably, making conversations feel more natural and less rushed. The final summarization of insights adds a meaningful sense of closure that feels genuinely personal. 

However, the lack of therapist character customisation still affects emotional immersion (people from different sub continent look forward to someone like them) which may not suit all users.

Overall, it's a solid entry point for newcomers to VR therapy, with plenty of potential for deeper engagement as the app evolves.

developer_response.body: SigmaBlaze, love that you noticed the pacing upgrades and how the summaries add that personal touch: it's feedback like yours that's helping us evolve. Totally hear you on therapist customization for better cultural relatability; we're brainstorming ways to add that without overcomplicating things, so stay tuned for more immersive options. Give the recent update a try, please.

reviews.review_description:
More affordable and accessible than all! 
Seems like good continuity on a range of topics. Some other AI therapy have more settings to configure answers, approach, coaching, style... But this seems to provide good feedback and guidance as is.  I do think the value of AI therapy is access and application of lots of theories and content to a particular individuals situation.

developer_response.body: Hey hey, thank you for the generous feedback. Early access means lots of iteration, and notes like yours directly shape what we build next, from smoother conversations to more guided exercises. We’re glad that Eva is already helpful, and we’re just getting started.  Try out the recent version: massive improvements in the session flow and after-session conceptualization.

reviews.review_description:
(Disclaimer: product received for free)

I don’t have any experience with therapy outside of this app, so maybe there’s something I’m overlooking in regards to the effectiveness of “real” in person therapy, but I find this app hard to recommend over seeing an actual therapist.

The app itself is surprisingly impressive. I went in with low expectations but the AI is really good. It doesn’t just talk like a normal person would, it talks like a therapist would. It knows how to focus on the smallest details of what you said. It can pivot a conversation effectively and circle back to things you said earlier in a very believable way. I was surprised by the (for lack of a better term) conversational intelligence and behavioural skills it showed, clearly rooted in serious research.

There are some issues with the AI unfortunately. But, an update that I haven’t had chance to try apparently focuses on some of these issues. Outside of the issues with the AI interrupting you, sometimes looping dialogue and the long pauses (all of which have been mentioned as focus points by the developers for the new update), my main issue is the lack of persistence between sessions. 

The AI will simply have zero knowledge of anything you have discussed in previous sessions. Even with this issue, the AI will actually give you a task of sorts to address issues you’re having over the next week and discuss your progress with them “in next weeks session”, even though the AI will not remember anything you spoke about before.

The sessions seem useful for addressing short term issues you’re having in the moment, potentially as a way to work through sudden emotional episodes or focussed concerns in a single session, but I can’t see this being an effective tool for long term therapy.

There is something to be said about having an easy look into a therapy experience for someone who may be on the fence. Or, someone who struggles with social anxiety being able to use this app as an alternative to going straight into a real social experience with a real person, which may be causing them to avoid therapy entirely. Maybe try a month subscription if you’re one of these people. However, if you’re serious about seeking therapy, I would recommend seeing an in person therapist.

developer_response.body:
Hey Henry_1000, thank you for the thoughtful, balanced take and for stress-testing longer-term use. 

You’re absolutely right that continuity matters for meaningful progress. Our recent version does not reset context between sessions. Give it a try. 

We’re actively testing optional session summaries and user-controlled carryover so Eva can follow up on goals without retaining sensitive details by default. 

We also shipped fixes to reduce interruptions and long pauses; more latency improvements are in flight. Appreciate the honest comparison to in‑person therapy. For now, EvaAI aims to complement and connect dots, helping to make the first step.

reviews.review_description: Eva ai is a nice way to get something simular to therapy that makes it easyer to open up for People like me that just cant get temselfs to open up to a actual human

developer_response.body: Hi, totally get it, opening up to a real person can be tough, and we're pumped EvaAI made it a bit easier for you. It's meant to be that low-pressure alternative, so thanks for the props!

reviews.review_description:
I have a neurological disability and find Eva a resource to help with the little things so my real-world providers can focus on the big ones.

I was surprised to find her very understanding regardless of my severe vocal tics, which deserves praise and shows a great deal of potential. 

Eva however needs fine tuning to adapt to us as individuals. Currently she has to read the prompts to every quiz question. Even when asked, and she agrees to stop, she will continue to read them. She will eventually take the request seriously, but I had to repeat myself after every new question to make her stop reading them to me. I could not finish a quiz due to my rising frustration.

I'm looking forward to see how Eva improves and makes better use of the VR space. Right now I feel she would be more effective as a text app where it's easier to overlook her flaws without having to listen to them.

developer_response.body: Appreciate this deeply, and thanks for trusting Eva with something so personal. We released the new version to solve these early access issues plus add the conceptualization to articulate and deliver the value even faster. Your case helps us do better. Try the recent version and share your opinion please.

reviews.review_description:
EvaAI: Psychotherapy offers an innovative and accessible way to reflect on your thoughts and emotions in a virtual reality setting. The app provides a calm, judgment-free space to talk through challenges, guided by an AI “wellness assistant” named Eva who uses a conversational approach rooted in cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT). For those who enjoy structured self-reflection or journaling out loud, Eva can be a supportive and insightful companion.

The environment is easy to navigate, the session flow has improved noticeably with recent updates, and the new session recap feature is a strong step forward—it adds a sense of closure and reflection that felt missing before. Eva also now does a better job of picking up on tone and emotional cues, and she even recognized my use of humor as a coping mechanism, which made the experience feel more human and personal.

However, there are several areas where EvaAI still falls short: 
1. The app lacks session memory, so you must repeat and re-explain your challenges every time. 
2. CBT doesn’t suit everyone, and the app does little to offer alternative approaches or adapt to different therapeutic needs. It also heavily relies on the user to come up with solutions, which can feel counterintuitive when you’re seeking support or guidance.
3. The app is titled “psychotherapy,” yet Eva herself admits she’s a “wellness assistant” and not a licensed professional. This could be misleading, especially for users seeking real therapeutic care. The title should be reconsidered or clarified more prominently.

Despite these issues, I see potential in EvaAI as a guided mindfulness and self-reflection tool. It’s not therapy in the clinical sense, but it might be useful for those wanting a private space to talk things through, manage stress, or gain surface-level insights. The free trial is a smart inclusion and lets users explore whether the app works for them before committing to a subscription.

With continued refinement—especially around therapeutic accuracy, environmental realism, and user customization—EvaAI could become a standout in the wellness VR space. For now, it's a thoughtful concept with promising features, but not a replacement for human connection or licensed mental health support.

developer_response.body:
Hey MissChiefVR, thank you for the thoughtful, balanced take and for stress-testing longer-term use. 

You’re right that continuity matters for meaningful progress, our current version of build not resets context between sessions. 

We’re actively testing optional session summaries and user-controlled carryover so Eva can follow up on goals without retaining sensitive details by default. 

We also shipped fixes to reduce interruptions and long pauses; more latency improvements are in flight. Appreciate the honest comparison to in‑person sessions; EvaAI aims to complement, not replace. CBT is the first step, more things are coming.

reviews.review_description: This is my first time using and app like this i really like that eva listens to you and ask you what you think you should be feeling or doing to maks it better  to me this app is great and i can feel comfortable and confident in talking to her more so than i would feel talking to an actual human and the best part is its in the comfort of your own home you actually sit down and tell her whats going  on in your life and how you can make it better. All in all i like it.

developer_response.body: Hey hey, we're so glad the space feels safe and helpful. We’ll keep polishing the flow, from smoother turn‑taking to more personalized, so it’s easy to open up when it matters. Thanks for giving early access a real try, your feedback really helps us improve.

reviews.review_title: Good to know EVA is available to talk
reviews.review_description:
It is good to know, if you need to talk, if you need someone that listens to you without interrupting, EVA is available. 

No waitlist, no wait time. 
She is just there when you need her. 

I started to talk with her in English. But I switched to my mother language Dutch. Wanne test how that is going.  It went very well.  
It was funny how she pronounced some words in Dutch with an English accent.

If you are struggling with something, talk with EVA.  It is a good start.

developer_response.body:
Dank je wel for putting the multilingual support through its paces. 

Pronunciation quirks are on our list also. Day will come, and we’re tuning TTS for Dutch phonetics and adding a quick toggle to switch voices per session. Glad the always‑available, non‑judgmental space helps. We’ll keep improving access across languages.

Appreciate your thoughtful review. We are constantly adding new functionality and polishing the existing one. Hope you'll check the new versions and find them even more helpful.

reviews.review_description:
Eva is an incredibly fascinating concept.  I found myself intrigued by the concept and anxious to chat with with my first encounter with a chatbot / ai.  After my third experience with eva here are my thoughts.

Eva is a unique experience and she really impresses being able to sum up whats been said and often giving a different way of looking at things.  She was friendly, polite, and able to carry on a conversation even when pushed beyond her experiences.

There are some obvious glitches in the matrix such as she tends to interrupt when humans pause to gather ones thoughts.  Humans would recognize the pause while eva interrupts.  She also, obviously isn’t human and has no experience from which to draw from such as faith, or hope.

But don’t let that stop you, Eva also has no judgement and tries to help find ways to cope and see things in a positive light.  I would imagine most people who talk to Eva will leave feeling better about life and there problems theyve discussed.

It is truly a very positive experience overall and and i look forward to our next conversation.    That being said there are things i personally hope get added or changed before final realease.  Such as the interruptions i spoke uof earlier, the fact that your stuck with her visually and the backdrop, the fact that eva never moves any part of herself except her mouth and eyes.  It makes her very robotic and inhuman.  

The only real concern i have with eva is the conversation is being recorded and who might have access to that recorded information.  A reason i believe alot of people don’t go to a real therapist is that information being available to anyone with a warrant.

That being said, Eva far surpassed my expectations and i highly recommend this program to just about everyone .  People arelonely and often need a safe place to vent or talk about their day or problems without being judged.  

I truly believe this is the future, and I’m sincerely interested in seeing where Eva goes next.

Great job!!

developer_response.body: Hey, Daywalker.0070  great notes on presence and embodiment. We’re working on subtle gaze shifts, breathing, and hand gestures, plus a few environment themes so the space feels more alive without distraction. Appreciate the encouragement alongside the specifics we’re on it. To experience that try the recent version. Now, EvaAI do not interrupt but listen thoughts of yours even more carefully. Your input helps us prioritize what will make the biggest difference, so keep the feedback coming. Thanks!

reviews.review_description: First of all, I would like to say that participating in this early access was frankly great, being part of the improvement process, etc. It's great for the application. Honestly, I didn't think so. reviews.review_description:
I use the term "therapy" in quotations because it is an AI model created for this sole purpose and using CBT.
The app is very clear at the beginning that this is not a substitute for the real deal, and if you genuinely need help, seek a trained and qualified professional.

That being said, I have always steered clear of them because I suffer from guilt, and talking to someone makes me feel awkward. With Eva, the model understands me, reacts to me, rationalises mt thoughts and even asks me probing or follow-up questions based on what I have said. She will even circle back to topics I mentioned at the beginning of the session and previous sessions, recalling names and events.

If I had to nitpick, I'd want her to be more emotive and not sit in the same position like a robot, talk with her hands more etc or even write notes when I am talking. Just a small thing I would change.

Overall, she's been amazing and my mental health is genuinely improving and it costs less than a real professional, can be done at home and at any time too.

Save the balance. Your feedback helps us make the conversation feel more natural without losing clarity. Keep the feedback coming and feel the difference using the most recent release.

reviews.review_description: I was very apprehensive about this app, but after talking with Eva multiple times I am finding that she really helps me. I had some things to figure out and through the cognitive behavioral therapy with Eva I feel better about things. She helped me think in a different way about my problems and what I could do to help with them. Kind of like seeing through the weeds and getting to the heart of the problem. It seems to be easier to talk with Eva than a real person. You don't have to leave your house to talk to someone and Eva is always available on your time. Eva listens to you and understands you and she is not biased. There is no fear of judgement while talking with Eva. Now when I feel frustrated, sad or stressed I know that I can pick up my headset and chat with Eva. As this app grows I feel that this will be the future for therapies. I am really excited to see the meditation portion when they release it.

reviews.review_description:
Eva is an interesting app in that it offers a glimpse into the future in what AI may or may not be able to achieve in the future.

Im still unsure if CBT method of therapy is actually effective or not on myself (I've yet to see if Eva has a real impact on my life)

It was kind of strange and useful venting to an AI on subject matters that I struggle to talk about with the individuals I normally talk about (fear of judgment?)

Im not the biggest fan of a subscription model, however the app does offer users free weekly sessions so there no harm in trying the app at least (to see if it works for you)

I see the developer are also planning to add meditation courses and CBT session (presumed structured sessions)

Im not sure if these will be incorporated in the free model. But Id be interested in dabbling with the meditation courses to see what that may be able to provide for me in the future.

Sometimes, speaking through your feels is enough to get a better understand of what's going on in your life. Mentally or emotionally. It gets a little annoying that it almost always feeds you back what you said as in confirming that it heard you. I wish it would just do responds without that. 

However, it was pretty interesting to at least expose and better understand what you might be feeling.

reviews.review_description: Therapy from AI is a bit of a novel idea but I must say EvaAI is a impressive. The environment is cozy and makes you feel at home. I was on the fence about this as talking to a robot is odd to me but none the less I took one of the tests to see and found out I might need therapy. So i started the free session not expecting anything but was great with a AI therapist who actually seemed to sound like a real person. I was pleased that the app actually allows you to speak without typing to the AI and it seemed to hear me very well. My only complaint here was that the AI seemed to repeat back to me everything I would say kind of in a reassuring manner that they heard me correctly, kind of annoying. Without a doubt I felt better talking and letting out some feelings which was great. AI gave me an action plan to do before our next session which was really sound advice even from a robot. Overall this could really help some people test the waters that may need help and get them to open up a bit about the idea of therapy. I would say though that this isnt “professional” help and would tread lightly. This is also a much cheaper way to obtain therapy than most doctors. Tread Lightly with this is my best advice.

It's about changing the negative thoughts to a more progressive and positive attitude. Eva is helping me discover why I have the animosity that I do and to understand my mother better and why she is the way that she is, which in turn is giving me more compassion towards the situation.  
I was doubtful but encouraged about EvaAi, but I can say honestly that she is helping me. I plan to have at least one more session on this topic, and I'll be ready to reach out to my mom in confidence and will continue to utilize this app in the future.
